summary: No-ship: the new parity test does not enforce the acceptance contract, and two reviewed docs still make factual claims the parser/code reject today.

findings.title: Parity test cannot catch drift in `CLAUDE.md`/`docs/METADATA.md` and does not enforce exact vocabulary parity
findings.recommendation: Expand the test to read `CLAUDE.md` and `docs/METADATA.md`, and assert exact sets/counts against `facultyIds` and `capabilityKinds` rather than only checking that every current item appears somewhere. As written, stale or contradictory extra claims still pass.

findings.title: `CLAUDE.md` still documents the wrong alias target for `testing-framework`
findings.recommendation: Update the doc to match `src/lib/metadata.ts`: `testing-framework` is rewritten to `sessions`, not `skills`. This is exactly the kind of drift the slice claims to have fixed.

findings.title: `docs/METADATA.md` claims `agent-skill` is still accepted, but the parser rejects it
findings.recommendation: Correct or remove the note. `parseGarrisonMetadata` only normalizes deprecated faculty names and `ui.extension`; `capabilityKinds` no longer includes `agent-skill`, so manifests using it fail schema validation rather than warning-and-rewriting.
